backend/main.mo: Main Motoko actor implementing the backend for a media-sharing platform, including user profiles, content metadata, playback queues, likes, and comments with access-control authorization and blob storage mixins.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report mapping implemented features (edit profile, delete content, delete account) to their corresponding frontend files, components, and test IDs with confidence ratings.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: JSON document containing one-sentence summaries of each frontend source file, describing their purpose and notable implementation details across the Arena media platform.
frontend/index.css: Tailwind base CSS file defining OKLCH-based design tokens for light and dark themes, including background, foreground, card, border, and chart color variables.
frontend/index.html: Minimal HTML entry point for the React app titled 'The Arena', mounting the app at the #root div and loading the main TSX module.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Root React component that sets up TanStack Router with Feed, Upload, and ContentDetail routes inside a Layout shell, and conditionally renders a ProfileSetupModal for newly authenticated users.
Comments section component that displays, adds, and deletes comments for a content item, resolving author display names via per-principal profile queries and showing delete controls only to comment owners.
frontend/src/components/ConfirmationDial: Reusable AlertDialog confirmation component with configurable title, description, confirm/cancel labels, loading state, and optional destructive styling.
frontend/src/components/ContentCard.tsx: Renders a media content card with thumbnail, file type badge, and action buttons (like, add to queue, delete) that navigates to the content detail page on click.
frontend/src/components/EditProfileDialo: A modal dialog that allows authenticated users to update their display name, pre-populated with their current profile name.
A drag-and-drop file input component that displays a file preview with name and size once a file is selected, supporting audio, video, and image categories.
frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx: Main app layout component providing a sticky header with navigation links, login/profile controls, and a queue panel toggle button with badge count.
frontend/src/components/LoginButton.tsx: A button component that handles Internet Identity login and logout, showing a loading spinner during authentication and clearing the React Query cache on logout.
frontend/src/components/MediaPlayer.tsx: A full-featured media player component supporting audio and video playback with controls for play/pause, seek, volume, repeat, skip, and next-track navigation.
frontend/src/components/ProfileMenu.tsx: Dropdown menu component for authenticated users that displays their display name and provides options to edit profile, log out, or delete their account with confirmation dialogs.
frontend/src/components/QueuePanel.tsx: Slide-in side panel that displays the playback queue, allowing users to reorder, remove, or clear items and navigate to content by clicking queue entries.
frontend/src/components/ThumbnailCreator: Component for selecting a thumbnail for uploaded media, supporting either frame capture from a video via a seek slider or direct image file upload.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: Custom hook that creates and caches an authenticated or anonymous ICP backend actor using React Query, invalidating dependent queries whenever the actor changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides an Internet Identity authentication context and hook, managing AuthClient creation, login flow, identity persistence, and logout via a React context provider.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Centralizes all React Query hooks for backend interactions, covering user profiles, content feed, views, comments, playback queue, and related CRUD mutations.
frontend/src/index.css: Defines global CSS for the Arena app, importing Outfit and Bebas Neue fonts, setting up Tailwind directives, and declaring dark-themed CSS custom properties (OKLCH colors) plus utility classes for neon glow effects and card hover animations.
frontend/src/main.tsx: React app entry point that mounts the app with QueryClientProvider for React Query and InternetIdentityProvider for ICP authentication wrapping the root App component.
frontend/src/pages/ContentDetailPage.tsx: Detail page for a single media item that displays a media player, metadata (views, likes, comments), queue controls, and a delete option for the owner, with view increment on load and comment section toggling.
frontend/src/pages/FeedPage.tsx: Main discovery feed page showing all content with filtering by type (all/music/video), sorting (recent/popular), and text search, rendered as a responsive card grid with a hero banner and skeleton loading states.
frontend/src/pages/ProfileSetupModal.tsx: Modal dialog shown to new users prompting them to enter an artist/creator name, which is saved via the saveCallerUserProfile mutation before granting access to the app.
frontend/src/pages/UploadPage.tsx: Upload page allowing authenticated users to select a media file (audio/video), provide a title and description, capture a thumbnail, and submit the content with upload progress tracking.
frontend/src/utils/urlParams.ts: Utility module providing helper functions to read URL query parameters from both standard and hash-based routes, persist them in sessionStorage, and clean sensitive parameters from the URL hash.
frontend/src/utils/videoFrameCapture.ts: Exports a utility function `captureVideoFrame` that draws the current frame of an HTMLVideoElement onto a canvas and returns it as a PNG Blob.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration for the frontend, defining custom fonts, OKLCH-based color tokens (including arena-specific and sidebar tokens), box shadows, border radii, and animation keyframes.
project_state.json: Tracks the project state for 'The Arena' platform, listing existing implemented features (edit profile, delete content, delete account) and pending new feature requests around user self-management.
